skill.name: cass
skill.description: CASS helps agents search local coding-agent session history across Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, Aider, ChatGPT, and related tools.
skill.summary: Search local coding-agent session history with CASS robot-mode commands.
skill.author: Dicklesworthstone

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many command, filesystem, network, and heuristic flags, but most command detections are Markdown examples rather than embedded executable code. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill encourages agents to search sensitive local history, use SSH-based remote sources, and optionally execute remote installer scripts. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Broad Local Agent History Indexing
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use CASS to unify hidden local session folders for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, ChatGPT, Aider, and other tools. This is a legitimate purpose, but those folders can contain private prompts, code, credentials in transcripts, workspace paths, and unencrypted legacy ChatGPT data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly lists hidden agent-history locations and describes indexing them. The risk is data exposure, but the behavior matches the stated search-memory purpose.

medium_findings.title: SSH-Based Remote Source Setup
medium_findings.description: The skill documents remote source setup that discovers hosts from SSH configuration, probes remotes, can optionally install CASS on remotes, and syncs session data locally. This creates material risk of unintended remote access, data movement, or host modification if an agent runs it without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SSH discovery, remote probing, optional installation, and sync behavior are stated directly. The risk depends on user authorization and host configuration.

medium_findings.title: Remote Installer Commands Execute Downloaded Scripts
medium_findings.description: The installation section recommends shell and PowerShell one-liners that download scripts from GitHub and execute them. This is a common installer pattern, but it is dangerous for agent-driven workflows because it executes remote code before local inspection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands explicitly pipe downloaded installer content into shell or PowerShell execution. The URL is a public GitHub source, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Environment Variable Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill documents environment variables including an encryption key for ChatGPT data. This is not direct secret access, but it tells agents which sensitive variable may exist and should be handled carefully.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable list is explicit, including CHATGPT_ENCRYPTION_KEY. The file does not contain code that reads or exfiltrates it.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Flags Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The many Ruby backtick, shell substitution, and command-execution detections are primarily inline Markdown commands or fenced examples for the CASS CLI. They are not embedded Ruby code or an executable script inside the skill file, but agents could still run the documented commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside Markdown prose or fenced bash examples. No standalone executable script file is present in the audited file structure.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flags Not Confirmed as Implementations
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography static findings were not confirmed as cryptographic implementations in the skill. The file discusses concepts such as AES-256-GCM encryption and SHA-256 hashes, but no code implements a weak algorithm here.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation text rather than crypto code. Some static line matches appear to be caused by prose, tables, or command examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: SSH-Based Remote Source Setup
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents remote source setup that discovers hosts from SSH configuration, probes remotes, can optionally install CASS on remotes, and syncs session data locally. This creates material risk of unintended remote access, data movement, or host modification if an agent runs it without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The SSH discovery, remote probing, optional installation, and sync behavior are stated directly. The risk depends on user authorization and host configuration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Installer Commands Execute Downloaded Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The installation section recommends shell and PowerShell one-liners that download scripts from GitHub and execute them. This is a common installer pattern, but it is dangerous for agent-driven workflows because it executes remote code before local inspection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands explicitly pipe downloaded installer content into shell or PowerShell execution. The URL is a public GitHub source, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

content.value_statement: Past coding-agent work is often scattered across local tools and hidden session folders. This skill teaches agents to use CASS robot mode to find prior fixes, decisions, and context quickly.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to run CASS searches with JSON or robot-mode output for agents.
Documents indexing, health checks, status checks, timelines, and diagnostics.
Explains filters for agent, workspace, dates, sources, result fields, and token limits.
Describes local connectors for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, ChatGPT, Aider, and other agents.
Covers optional remote source setup, synchronization, and path mappings for multi-machine search.
Documents TUI features for human users and robot-mode contracts for automated use.
limitations: Requires the external CASS command to be installed and available on the system path.
Search quality depends on local session files, indexes, permissions, and connector support.
The skill can expose sensitive local agent conversations if used without access controls.
Remote source setup depends on SSH access and may require careful manual review.

use_cases.title: Recover a Past Fix
use_cases.description: Find earlier sessions that solved a similar error, migration issue, or test failure.

use_cases.title: Prepare Agent Handoffs
use_cases.description: Search recent work and summarize relevant session context before another agent continues.

use_cases.title: Audit Multi-Agent Work
use_cases.description: Review activity across several agent tools, workspaces, and time windows from one interface.

prompt_templates.title: Find Recent Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search for recent related work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use CASS robot mode to search my recent local agent sessions for this topic: [topic]. Limit the results and summarize the most useful matches.

prompt_templates.title: Search One Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Search CASS for [error or feature] within [workspace path]. Return only concise matches with file paths, line numbers, agents, and short explanations.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Agent Histories
prompt_templates.prompt: Use CASS to compare what Claude Code, Codex, and other local agents recorded about [topic]. Group the findings by agent and date.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Debugging Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a CASS health check, search for [incident], expand the best matches, and produce a debugging brief with assumptions, prior fixes, and remaining risks.

output_examples.input: Search for authentication timeout fixes from the last month.
output_examples.output: A concise list of matching sessions with agents, dates, source paths, relevant lines, and a short summary of the likely fix.

output_examples.input: Find what previous agents said about the database migration.
output_examples.output: A grouped summary showing the main sessions, the agent that produced each one, and the migration decisions found in context.

output_examples.input: Review today's agent activity for unresolved bugs.
output_examples.output: A timeline-style summary of recent sessions, unresolved issues, and suggested follow-up searches.

best_practices: Use robot or JSON mode for agent workflows so output is machine-readable.
Start with health and index checks before relying on search results.
Limit fields, result counts, and content length when working inside small context windows.
anti_patterns: Do not run bare CASS in an automated agent session because it can open an interactive TUI.
Do not index or search private session histories without user consent.
Do not run remote source setup or installer commands without reviewing the target hosts and scripts.

faq.question: What does this skill help an agent do?
faq.answer: It helps an agent use CASS to search local coding-agent history and retrieve relevant past work.

faq.question: Does this skill include the CASS application?
faq.answer: No. It documents how to use the external CASS command when it is installed.

faq.question: Why does it require robot or JSON mode?
faq.answer: Robot and JSON modes produce structured output that agents can parse without opening the interactive TUI.

faq.question: Can it search multiple agent tools?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation lists connectors for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, ChatGPT, Aider, and other tools.

faq.question: Does it send data to cloud services?
faq.answer: The skill describes local search by default, but optional remote sources use SSH-based synchronization.

faq.question: What should users review before using it?
faq.answer: Users should review indexed folders, SSH sources, installer commands, and any sensitive session data before running CASS.
